name: Adventist Chaplaincy Ministries
intro: Adventist Chaplaincy Ministries (ACM) cares for members and neighbours in hospitals, prisons, campuses, and the workplace — bringing comfort, prayer, and a listening ear wherever people are hurting or searching.

resources.title: Request a hospital or home visit

name: Children's Ministry
intro: Children's Ministry nurtures the youngest members of our church family through Sabbath programs, Bible adventures, and activities that plant the love of Jesus deep in young hearts.

resources.title: Register your child for Vacation Bible School

name: Communication
intro: The Communication department keeps our church family informed and connected — running the website, social media, live streams, and the weekly bulletin so no one misses what God is doing among us.

resources.title: Submit an announcement for the bulletin

name: Education
intro: The Education department champions Christ-centred learning — supporting Adventist schooling options, scholarships, and lifelong Bible-based education for every age.

name: Health Ministries
intro: Health Ministries shares God's plan for whole-person wellbeing — hosting health screenings, cooking classes, and programs built on the Adventist message of healthful living.

name: Public Affairs & Religious Liberty
intro: Public Affairs & Religious Liberty (PARL) defends freedom of conscience for all people and represents the church's voice on matters of faith, government, and human rights.

name: Publishing
intro: The Publishing department shares literature evangelism — placing Bibles, books, and study guides into the hands of people searching for hope and truth.

name: Personal Ministries
intro: Personal Ministries equips every member for everyday outreach — visitation, Bible studies, and small acts of witness that carry the gospel into daily life.

resources.title: Sign up to give a Bible study

name: Sabbath School
intro: Sabbath School anchors our week in God's word through small-group Bible study, gathering every Sabbath morning to search the scriptures together.

resources.title: Find a Sabbath School class near you

study_guides.title: [Placeholder] Current Quarter Study Guide
study_guides.blurb: The full lesson guide for this quarter, chapter by chapter.

study_guides.title: [Placeholder] Adult Bible Study Guide
study_guides.blurb: This week's adult Sabbath School lesson, ready to study.

study_guides.title: [Placeholder] Youth Study Guide
study_guides.blurb: A youth-focused companion guide to this quarter's lesson.

study_guides.title: [Placeholder] Children's Study Guide
study_guides.blurb: Simplified lesson content for younger learners.

name: Youth Ministries
intro: Youth Ministries walks alongside children, teens, and young adults through four age-based ministries — Adventurers, Pathfinders, Ambassadors, and Young Adults — making faith real at every stage.

subpages.name: Adventurers
subpages.intro: Adventurers welcomes children ages 6–9 into a Christ-centred club of badges, crafts, and outdoor fun, building character alongside their families.

subpages.name: Pathfinders
subpages.intro: Pathfinders guides youth ages 10–15 through skills, service, and Bible-based adventure — the church's answer to scouting, rooted in faith.

subpages.name: Ambassadors
subpages.intro: Ambassadors supports older teens (16–21) as they mature in leadership, service, and personal walk with Christ before stepping into adulthood.

subpages.name: Young Adults
subpages.intro: Young Adults creates space for college-age and working young adults to wrestle with faith, build community, and find their place serving the church.

name: Women's Ministries
intro: Women's Ministries builds community among the women of our church through prayer retreats, mentorship, and fellowship that encourages every woman in her walk with God.

name: Adventist Men Organization (AMO)
intro: The Adventist Men Organization unites the men of the church for fellowship, mentorship, and service — encouraging men to lead faithfully at home and in the church.

name: Family Ministries
intro: Family Ministries strengthens marriages, parenting, and home life through seminars, counselling referrals, and events that help families grow together in faith.

name: Stewardship Ministries
intro: Stewardship Ministries teaches faithful, joyful management of time, talent, and treasure — helping members grow in generosity as an act of worship.

name: Ministerial Association
intro: The Ministerial Association supports our pastoral team and equips lay leaders for effective, Christ-like ministry across the congregation.

name: Planned Giving & Trust Services
intro: Planned Giving & Trust Services helps members steward their resources wisely, offering free guidance on wills, estate planning, and legacy gifts that further God's work.

name: Possibility Ministries
intro: Possibility Ministries ensures our church is a welcoming, accessible home for people with disabilities and special needs — because everyone has a place at God's table.

name: Spirit of Prophecy Ministries
intro: Spirit of Prophecy Ministries promotes the writings of Ellen G. White as a source of counsel and encouragement, helping members read and apply them alongside scripture.

resources.title: Browse Spirit of Prophecy books online

name: Music Ministries
intro: Music Ministries leads the congregation in worship through choir, praise team, and instrumental music, offering our best gifts of song back to God.

resources.title: Join the choir or praise team
Request music for a special program

name: Deaconry
intro: The Deaconry serves the practical needs of the church — ushering, communion preparation, facilities, and care for members — with quiet, faithful service.

resources.title: Volunteer to serve as a deacon

name: Deaconess Ministry
intro: The Deaconess Ministry cares for the practical and spiritual needs of members — hospitality, communion preparation, and visitation — with a gentle, serving heart.

resources.title: Volunteer to serve as a deaconess

name: Elders Ministry
intro: The Elders Ministry provides spiritual oversight and pastoral support alongside the pastor — preaching, praying, and shepherding the congregation.

name: Treasury/Finance
intro: The Treasury/Finance department faithfully manages tithes, offerings, and church funds with transparency and biblical accountability.

name: Church Clerk/Secretariat
intro: The Church Clerk/Secretariat keeps official church records — membership, minutes, and correspondence — serving as the administrative backbone of the congregation.

name: Community Services
intro: Community Services extends practical Christian care to our neighbourhood through two arms — Dorcas and Adventist Community Services — clothing, feeding, and comforting those in need.

subpages.name: Dorcas
subpages.intro: The Dorcas Society sews, mends, and distributes clothing and household goods to families in need, following the biblical example of Tabitha's ministry of kindness.

subpages.name: Adventist Community Services
subpages.intro: Adventist Community Services (ACS) provides disaster response, food assistance, and neighbourhood outreach, meeting practical needs in Jesus' name.

name: Evangelism Ministry
intro: The Evangelism Ministry plans and runs public meetings, small-group outreach, and revival series to introduce our community to Jesus and His soon return.

name: Prayer Ministry
intro: The Prayer Ministry covers our church in intercession — leading prayer meetings, prayer chains, and seasons of corporate prayer and fasting.

name: Sabbath School Branch Sabbath School
intro: Our Branch Sabbath School organises age-based divisions for children, giving every child a Sabbath School class suited to how they learn and grow.

subpages.name: Cradle Roll
subpages.intro: Cradle Roll welcomes our tiniest members (birth to age 2) with songs, cuddles, and simple Bible stories shared alongside parents.

subpages.name: Kindergarten
subpages.intro: Kindergarten (ages 3–5) introduces preschoolers to Jesus through stories, songs, crafts, and play in a warm, nurturing classroom.

subpages.name: Primary
subpages.intro: Primary (ages 6–9) helps early elementary children explore Bible stories and begin building their own relationship with God.

subpages.name: Juniors
subpages.intro: Juniors (ages 10–12) dig deeper into scripture, memory verses, and mission stories as children mature in their faith.

subpages.name: Earliteens
subpages.intro: Earliteens (ages 13–14) tackles real-life questions of faith as young teens transition toward the youth division.
